Colorado Pronghorn Antelope · Moffat County

Record-Class Pronghorn

Northwest Colorado — Moffat County in particular — is known for its record-class pronghorn antelope. Herds of a hundred or more roam our sagebrush canyons and rolling hills.

100%Opportunity
70sBuck class (mid–high)
100+Herd size
57,000Private acres
ZeroApplications needed
The Hunt

An experience of a lifetime

Northwestern Colorado has distinguished itself for record-class pronghorn antelope — Moffat County in particular. Canyons and rolling hills filled with sagebrush and draws are the perfect backdrop for this fast, exciting hunt.

We have an abundant population of antelope, with herds of a hundred or more. Our pronghorns run from the mid to high 70s, and our guides will create the experience of a lifetime as you stalk these sharp-eyed speedsters across open country.

With guaranteed licenses through Big Gulch Ranching for Wildlife and 100% opportunity, antelope is one of the most rewarding hunts on the ranch — and a perfect combo with elk or deer.

100%
Opportunity
Open Country · Spot & Stalk

Sagebrush & open plains

Pronghorn hunting is a different rhythm than elk or deer — wide-open terrain, long glassing, and careful stalks on animals that can see for miles and run faster than anything else on the continent.

Our guides have spent years reading this country. They'll help you close the distance and pick out the mature bucks from the herd.

Moffat County · Record Class

Trophy country

Moffat County has produced some of Colorado's finest pronghorn, and our corner of it is no exception. The mix of canyons, draws, and rolling sage gives antelope room to grow heavy horns.

Mid-to-high 70s bucks are the standard here, with herds big enough that you'll have your pick. Add a pronghorn to an elk or deer hunt for a true Colorado combo.

Fully Outfitted

Everything's handled

01

Lodging

Comfortable accommodations at the ranch headquarters. You arrive the evening before your hunt begins — no long drives to camp each morning.

02

All Meals

Home-cooked meals throughout your stay. We keep hunters well-fed and fueled for long days in the field.

03

Guaranteed License

Your license comes through the Ranching for Wildlife program. No draw, no application, no waiting — it's guaranteed.

04

Expert Guiding

Two hunters per guide. Every guide is intimately familiar with the ranch and its game.

05

Transportation

Travel to and from the hunting areas is provided. No need to navigate 57,000 acres on your own.

06

Game Care

Proper field dressing and care of your animal after the harvest, so your meat and cape make it home in top condition.
